items.summary: Single-seed explore handler assembles domain config manually while multi-seed passes RelationConfig directly to the service

evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore (lines ~82-95): extracts bcqlPattern, reverseCollocatePattern, headPos, collocatePos from RelationConfig, constructs ExploreOptions, then calls semanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern(seed, name, bcql, reversePattern, opts) — 5 arguments extracted from config
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: calls semanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed(seeds, relationConfig.get(), minLogDice, topCollocates, minShared) — passes RelationConfig directly
The handler even has a TODO comment acknowledging that domain object assembly should be delegated to a factory/service
This means evolving RelationConfig requires coordinated changes to the handler (single-seed) but not the service (multi-seed)
detail.suggestion: Add an overload or refactor exploreByPattern to accept RelationConfig directly (mirroring exploreMultiSeed), and move bcqlPattern/reversePattern/position extraction into the service layer. This removes the domain-assembly responsibility from the HTTP handler.

action_steps.title: Wrap single-relation sketch collocations in per-relation object shape to match full-sketch endpoint structure (9fbcd7f8)
action_steps.detail: SketchHandlers.java single-relation endpoint: wrap returned collocations list in Map.of('relation', relName, 'collocations', list) matching the per-relation object shape of the full-sketch response

action_steps.title: Add RelationConfig-accepting overload to exploreByPattern mirroring multi-seed path; remove manual config assembly from single-seed handler (db78d2e0)
action_steps.detail: SemanticFieldExplorer.java: add exploreByPattern(RelationConfig, String, int) overload; ExplorationHandlers.java single-seed handler: replace manual config assembly with this new overload

items.summary: Full-sketch and single-relation sketch endpoints return structurally incompatible response shapes

evidence: handleFullSketchImpl returns {lemma, relations: {id: {name, cql, collocations:[...]}}} — collocations nested two levels deep under a relation-keyed map
handleRelationQueryInternal returns {lemma, relation, collocations:[...]} — collocations flat at the top level, no per-relation metadata
Both are served under /api/sketch/{lemma} and /api/sketch/{lemma}/{relation} — a client switching between the two must handle two incompatible schemas
detail.suggestion: Have the single-relation endpoint wrap its collocations inside the same per-relation object shape as the full endpoint: {lemma, relations: {<id>: {name, cql, collocations:[...]}}}. This makes the single-relation response a predictable subset of the full-sketch response.
